Combat sport events in 2024
ONE Fight Night 26: Lee vs. Rasulov
The poster for ONE Fight Night 26: Lee vs. Rasulov
PromotionONE Championship
DateDecember 7, 2024 (2024-12-07)
VenueLumpinee Boxing Stadium
CityBangkok, Thailand
Event chronology
ONE Friday Fights 90: Kongklai vs. Kacem ONE Fight Night 26: Lee vs. Rasulov ONE Friday Fights 91: Komawut vs. Balyko

ONE Fight Night 26: Lee vs. Rasulov was a combat sports event produced by ONE Championship that took place on December 7, 2024, at Lumpinee Boxing Stadium in Bangkok, Thailand.[1][2][3]

Background

[edit]

A ONE Lightweight World Championship bout between current champion (also the ONE Welterweight Champion and ONE Lightweight World Grand Prix winner) Christian Lee and Alibeg Rasulov headlined the event.[4] The bout was originally scheduled for ONE 169, but was moved to this event for unknown reasons.[5]

A ONE Women's Atomweight Submission Grappling World Championship rematch between current champion (also multiple-time IBJJF World Champion) Mayssa Bastos and former champion Danielle Kelly took place at the event.[6] They previously met at ONE Fight Night 24 in August 2024, where Bastos won the title by unanimous decision.[7]

Cole Abate made his promotional debut against former two-time ONE Lightweight World Champion Shinya Aoki in a lightweight submission grappling match at the event.[8]

Diego Paez was expected to make his promotional debut against Nakrob Fairtex in a flyweight muay thai bout.[9] However, Paez withdrew due to injury and was replaced by Kongthoranee Sor.Sommai.[10]

At the weigh-ins, former ONE Bantamweight Kickboxing World Champion Petchtanong Petchfergus failed hydration and missed weight for his bantamweight kickboxing bout against Nabil Anane and the bout was cancelled when Petchtanong was hospitalized and required IV drips. Denis Purić and Elias Mahmoudi were missed weight over the flyweight limit and the bout met in a catchweight bout at 138.75 pounds.[11]

Results

[edit]
Fight card (Amazon Prime Video)
Weight class Method Round Time Notes
Lightweight Christian Lee (c) vs. Alibeg Rasulov No Contest (accidental eye poke) 2 4:55 [a]
Flyweight Muay Thai Kongthoranee Sor.Sommai def. Nakrob Fairtex Decision (unanimous) 3 3:00
Women's Atomweight Submission Grappling Mayssa Bastos (c) def. Danielle Kelly Decision (unanimous) 1 10:00 [b]
Flyweight Reece McLaren def. Jarred Brooks Decision (split) 3 5:00
Flyweight Muay Thai Elias Mahmoudi vs. Denis Purić No Contest (overturned) 3 2:58 [c]
Flyweight Yuya Wakamatsu def. Gilbert Nakatani Decision (unanimous) 3 5:00
Featherweight Shamil Gasanov def. Halil Amir Decision (unanimous) 3 5:00
Lightweight Submission Grappling Cole Abate def. Shinya Aoki Submission (heel hook) 1 2:25
Strawweight Kickboxing Jonathan Di Bella def. Rui Botelho Decision (unanimous) 3 3:00
Lightweight Submission Grappling Dante Leon def. Bruno Pucci Submission (triangle armbar) 1 2:01
Strawweight Muay Thai Thongpoon P.K.Saenchai def. Danial Williams TKO (punches) 2 1:10
  1. ^ For the ONE Lightweight World Championship. Accidental eye poke rendered Rasulov unable to continue.
  2. ^ For the ONE Women's Atomweight Submission Grappling World Championship.
  3. ^ Both fighter missed weight: Mahmoudi (138.75 lb) and Purić (138 lb). Originally a KO (knees to the body and punches) win for Mahmoudi; overturned after he tested positive for meldonium.[12]

[13]

Bonus awards

[edit]

The following fighters received $50,000 bonuses.[14]

  • Performance of the Night: Cole Abate, Dante Leon and Thongpoon P.K.Saenchai
